title: Cisco Extended ACLs
description: Learn advanced extended IP access control lists including protocol and port filtering, named ACLs, logging, established connections, and interface application for granular traffic control.

tasks.prompt: Create extended ACL 100 to permit TCP traffic from host 10.1.1.10 to host 10.1.2.20 on destination port 80.
aliases: access-list 100 permit tcp host 10.1.1.10 host 10.1.2.20 eq 80
tasks.hint: Use: 'access-list 100 permit tcp host 10.1.1.10 host 10.1.2.20 eq 80'
tasks.explanation: Extended ACLs filter on source/destination IP, protocol, and port numbers. The 'eq 80' keyword matches HTTP traffic.

tasks.prompt: Deny all TCP traffic to any destination on port 23 (Telnet) in extended ACL 100.
aliases: access-list 100 deny tcp any any eq 23
tasks.hint: Use: 'access-list 100 deny tcp any any eq 23'
tasks.explanation: Blocking Telnet (port 23) is a common security practice. Extended ACLs can target specific services regardless of source or destination IP.

tasks.prompt: Permit UDP traffic from any source to any destination on port 53 (DNS) in extended ACL 100.
aliases: access-list 100 permit udp any any eq 53
tasks.hint: Use: 'access-list 100 permit udp any any eq 53'
tasks.explanation: UDP port 53 is used for DNS queries. Extended ACLs allow protocol-specific filtering beyond what standard ACLs support.

tasks.prompt: Permit all ICMP traffic from network 10.1.1.0/24 to any destination in extended ACL 100.
tasks.explanation: ICMP is commonly permitted for ping and traceroute diagnostics. Extended ACLs support ICMP as a filterable protocol.

tasks.prompt: Permit TCP return traffic for established sessions from any source to network 10.1.1.0/24 in ACL 100.
tasks.explanation: The 'established' keyword matches TCP segments with the ACK flag set, allowing return traffic for outbound-initiated connections.

tasks.prompt: Permit all IP traffic from 172.16.0.0/16 to 172.17.0.0/16 in extended ACL 100.
tasks.explanation: Using 'ip' as the protocol permits all IP-based traffic between two subnets, useful as a broad allow rule placed after specific denies.

tasks.prompt: Deny all IP traffic from host 10.1.1.99 to any destination and log matched packets in ACL 100.
aliases: access-list 100 deny ip host 10.1.1.99 any log
tasks.hint: Use: 'access-list 100 deny ip host 10.1.1.99 any log'
tasks.explanation: The 'log' keyword generates a syslog message for each packet matching the rule, aiding security monitoring and incident investigation.

tasks.prompt: Create a named extended ACL called 'WEB-FILTER'.
tasks.explanation: Named ACLs use descriptive identifiers instead of numbers, making them easier to manage and reference in large configurations.

tasks.prompt: In named ACL 'WEB-FILTER', permit TCP traffic from any source to any destination on port 443.
tasks.explanation: Within named ACL configuration mode, rules are entered without the access-list prefix. Port 443 is used for HTTPS encrypted web traffic.

tasks.prompt: In named ACL 'WEB-FILTER', deny all remaining IP traffic.
tasks.explanation: An explicit deny ip any any at the end of a named ACL provides a clear deny-all policy, though an implicit deny already exists.

tasks.prompt: Add a remark to named ACL 'WEB-FILTER' stating 'Allow HTTPS only'.
tasks.explanation: Remarks in named ACLs document the intent of subsequent rules, improving readability for operations and audit teams.

tasks.prompt: Apply extended ACL 100 inbound on interface GigabitEthernet0/0.
tasks.explanation: Extended ACLs should be placed as close to the source as possible. Applying inbound filters traffic before it enters the network.

tasks.prompt: Apply named ACL 'WEB-FILTER' outbound on interface GigabitEthernet0/1.
tasks.explanation: Named ACLs are applied to interfaces the same way as numbered ACLs, using the ACL name instead of a number.

tasks.prompt: Display the contents and hit counters for extended ACL 100.
tasks.explanation: Viewing a specific ACL shows each rule and its match count, helping verify that traffic is being filtered as intended.

tasks.prompt: Remove the named extended ACL 'WEB-FILTER' from the router.
tasks.explanation: Named extended ACLs are removed with 'no ip access-list extended' followed by the ACL name. Detach it from interfaces first.
